summary: Migrated Jigglypuff/Purin special-move code out of the monolithic ftpurin assembly file into src/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftpurin.c, partially matching several functions while preserving many remaining functions as MWERKS inline asm. The PR removed most text from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpurin.s, kept data/action-state table material there, added a large ftpurin.h prototype surface, and exposed small cross-system prototypes needed by the migrated C. The author noted the PR depended on #706 and resolved #685; a later comment clarified the file was migrated but matching functions were still missing, so this was intentionally partial.
what_changed: Added a large C implementation file section for ftPurin, increasing src/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftpurin.c by 6890 lines.
Deleted 6469 lines from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpurin.s while leaving the as_table_purin data/action-state table and constants/data-related material in assembly.

Matched or expressed in normal C the beginning of the migrated special-hi path, including lbl_8013C94C, ftPurin_SpecialHi_SetVars, ftPurin_SpecialHi_SetActionFromFacingDirection, ftPurin_SpecialHi_StartAction, ftPurin_SpecialAirHi_StartAction, and func_8013CD34.

Kept many unmatched routines in ftpurin.c as MWERKS_GEKKO inline asm blocks using asm void functions, nofralloc, and clang-format-off comments, preserving exact code while moving ownership to the source file.

Added callback/effect logic for Purin's SpecialHi accessory behavior in C: spawning effect 1238 once via ef_Spawn, assigning efLib_PauseAll and efLib_ResumeAll hitlag callbacks, and clearing x21BC_callback_Accessory4.
Added helper-style C factoring around SpecialHi action choice based on facing_dir and action-state IDs 365/367 for ground and 366/368 for air.

Added ftpurin.h prototypes for many ftPurin functions and labels from the migrated range; the diff evidence shows some duplicate declarations, so the header was more of a migration surface than a fully cleaned API.
Added one-prototype header dependencies: func_8016B1D8 in code_801601C4.h, func_801D4FF8 in grstadium.h, and func_800569EC in mplib.h.
smart_moves: Used a hybrid migration strategy: move the file into C now, match what is tractable, and embed remaining routines as inline asm so future work can replace individual functions incrementally without keeping the whole text section in asm.

Factored repeated SpecialHi facing-direction action-state selection into a small inline helper instead of duplicating the left/right Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC branch in both ground and air start actions.
Introduced local helper ftPurin_SpecialHi_SetVars to isolate repeated state setup for x2200_ftcmd_var0, x21BC_callback_Accessory4, and x2340_stateVar1.
Preserved exact compiler/assembler behavior for unmatched code with MWERKS_GEKKO asm wrappers and nofralloc rather than attempting risky approximate C.
Added the missing declarations in the owning headers for cross-system calls used by the migrated C, reducing reliance on implicit declarations.
decomp_lessons: For very large fighter files, a partial migration can be useful even when many functions remain unmatched: it centralizes the file in src/, exposes prototypes, and creates smaller future matching targets.

When replacing assembly with C, keep data/action-state tables in assembly if their layout is not yet represented by a reliable C struct or initializer; this PR left as_table_purin in ftpurin.s while moving text routines.
Inline helpers can improve matching attempts by controlling repeated instruction patterns, but the evidence here is only for the SpecialHi setup path, not for all Purin specials.
Unmatched functions can be carried forward as inline asm in the C file under MWERKS_GEKKO, letting the build continue to match while allowing gradual C replacement.
Header churn during migration can include broad or duplicate prototypes; future cleanup should verify exported symbols and remove duplicates once the function set is better understood.
Effect callbacks and fighter callback fields are important matching anchors: here x21D4/x21D8 hitlag callbacks and x21BC accessory callbacks explain several stores from the old assembly.

The author's comment that the file was migrated but matching functions were missing is a reminder to distinguish source migration from actual function matching progress in PR titles and tracking issues.
naming_conventions: Known exported fighter entrypoints kept character-prefixed names such as ftPurin_SpecialHi_StartAction, ftPurin_SpecialAirHi_StartAction, ftPurin_SpecialLw_StartAction, ftPurin_SpecialAirLw_StartAction, ftPurin_SpecialS_StartAction, ftPurin_SpecialAirS_StartAction, ftPurin_SpecialN_StartAction, and ftPurin_SpecialAirN_StartAction.
Unknown routines retained address-label names such as lbl_8013CB68, lbl_8013CBE4, lbl_8013CC60, and lbl_8014222C.
Unknown but callable non-label routines retained func_8013xxxx names such as func_8013CD34, func_8013CDD8, func_8013D104, func_8013D19C, func_8013D590, and func_8013D5F0.
New local helpers used descriptive Purin-specific names such as ftPurin_SpecialHi_SetVars and ftPurin_SpecialHi_SetActionFromFacingDirection.
The diff uses the project convention HSD_GObj* fighter_gobj for fighter object parameters and Fighter* fp/fighter for user data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the old standalone text assembly for ftpurin while retaining many exact routines as inline asm blocks inside ftpurin.c.
Wrapped inline asm with #ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO and #pragma push, using asm void signatures and nofralloc to preserve register/stack behavior.
Used extern const float/double labels such as lbl_804D9C10, lbl_804D9C14, lbl_804D9C18, lbl_804D9C20, lbl_804D9C30, and others instead of immediately replacing all rodata with named constants.
Matched branches around facing_dir by comparing against lbl_804D9C10 and selecting action IDs, matching the old assembly pattern for SpecialHi and transition helpers.
Kept action-state table entries in asm but rewrote some table literals from .4byte hexadecimal to .int and .float forms, e.g. .int 295 and .float 0.0.
Used exact callback/function symbols from adjacent systems, including ef_Spawn, efLib_PauseAll, efLib_ResumeAll, Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC, func_8006EBA4, func_8016B1D8, and func_801D4FF8.

review_feedback.feedback: PR declared dependency on #706 and that it resolves #685.
review_feedback.impact: Signals this migration was part of a staged ftpurin effort and should be understood with adjacent PRs/issues.

review_feedback.feedback: The author re-drafted after realizing they were looking at the wrong commit, clarifying: "The file is migrated but the matching functions are missing."
review_feedback.impact: Important scope clarification: this PR is primarily migration plus partial matches, not a complete ftpurin match.

review_feedback.feedback: Approved with no body or inline review comments in the provided slice.
review_feedback.impact: No detailed reviewer rationale is available from the dump.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace ftpurin inline asm functions with matched C, especially lbl_8013CB68 through lbl_8014222C.
Search for cleanup of duplicate ftpurin.h prototypes introduced by this migration.
Inspect issue #685 and PR #706 to understand the staged migration context for ftPurin.
Compare later changes to as_table_purin to see when the action-state table was moved from asm into typed C data.
Search for naming upgrades from lbl_8013xxxx/func_8013xxxx to semantic ftPurin special-move names.
